growth_records.medium: chemically defined medium, glucose as sole carbon source

up.method: experimental batch cultivation (values cited from Fallet et al. 2010, ref 70); range of measured specific glucose uptake rates at varying glucose concentrations, used to validate model growth flux (in silico vs experimental growth agreed within 1.5-2.5% error for 3.307, 3.478 and 3.751 mmol/gDW/h)

sec.method: experimental batch cultivation result reported by Fallet et al. 2010 (ref 70), glucose as sole carbon source; the paper's own FBA at 1.5 mmol/gDW/h glucose uptake predicted 0.75 mmol/gDW/h ectoine, compared against this experimental 0.72

product_yields.ectoine_on_glucose_in_silico: 49-50% mmol ectoine/mmol glucose (FBA-predicted range at 3-10 mmol/gDW/h glucose uptake; not experimental)

growth_records.cite: Ates, Ozlem et al. Genome-scale reconstruction of metabolic network for a halophilic extremophile, Chromohalobacter salexigens DSM 3043. BMC Systems Biology (2011). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-5-12. [PMC3034673]
growth_records.snippet: Experimental and in silico growth fluxes for batch cultivation of C. salexigens at varying glucose uptake rates (3.193 - 3.751 mmol/gDW/h)... The performed FBA simulations with 1.5 mmol/gDW/h glucose uptake resulted in an ectoine production
growth_records.notes: This is a genome-scale reconstruction (iOA584) paper; nearly all rates in it are FBA/in silico predictions, NOT measured here. The only EXPERIMENTAL prokaryote rates are borrowed from reference 70 = Fallet C, Rohe P, Franco-Lara E, 'Process optimization of the integrated synthesis and secretion of ectoine and hydroxyectoine under hyper/hypo-osmotic stress', Biotechnol Bioeng, 2010. Cited experimental values: (a) measured specific glucose uptake rates for batch cultivation spanning 3.193, 3.307, 3.478, 3.751 mmol/gDW/h (individual per-concentration growth rates are shown only in Figure 3, not given numerically in the text); (b) experimental ectoine production of 0.72 mmol/gDW/h with glucose as sole carbon source. Medium described only as 'chemically defined media with glucose as sole carbon source'; no numeric composition, temperature, or pH given here. In-silico-only figures deliberately EXCLUDED: e.g. isoleucine 1 mmol/gDW/h -> 0.129 h-1; fructose 1 mmol/gDW/h -> 0.1290 h-1; sucrose 1 mmol/gDW/h -> 0.0646 h-1; ectoine yield 49-50% and biomass 0.1934-0.642 h-1 over 3-10 mmol/gDW/h glucose; choline-stimulation growth 0.065->0.129 h-1 -- all FBA predictions, not experimental.
